name: Field
goal: Semantic wrapper that binds a label, help text, error text, and any form control into one a11y-correct unit.
description: Gateway form-field wrapper. Renders a <label> wired to the slotted control, optional help text (aria-describedby), and error text (aria-invalid + aria-describedby). Auto-generates control ids when absent. Required reflects to native required or aria-required. Does not participate in form submission itself.

props.name: label
props.description: Label text. Overridden by slot="label" for rich content.

props.name: help
props.description: Help text shown below the control when no error is present. Overridden by slot="help".

props.name: error
props.description: Error text shown below the control. When set, aria-invalid=true is applied to the control. Overridden by slot="error".

props.name: required
props.description: Reflects the required constraint onto the bound control (native required attribute or aria-required for custom elements).

props.name: controlId
props.description: Explicit id of the control to bind. If absent the first non-named-slot child element is auto-discovered and assigned a generated id.

slots.description: The form control (ce-input, native input, or any interactive element).

slots.name: label
slots.description: Rich label override; replaces the label attribute.

slots.name: help
slots.description: Rich help text override; replaces the help attribute.

slots.name: error
slots.description: Rich error text override; replaces the error attribute.

cssVariables.name: --ce-color-red
cssVariables.description: Error text color and required asterisk color.

sideEffects.description: Mutates slotted control: sets id, aria-describedby, aria-invalid, required/aria-required.
sideEffects.reason: a11y wiring requires the label and description associations to be present on the actual control element, not the wrapper.

a11y.role: none (the wrapper is presentational; the control inside carries its own role)
a11y.notes: ce-field wires label[for], aria-describedby, aria-invalid, and required/aria-required onto the bound control. The label element is rendered in shadow DOM. Id references across the shadow boundary work correctly in all modern browsers because id-based aria references are resolved in the flat tree. The error region uses role=alert + aria-live=polite so screen readers announce errors when they appear reactively.
